scope: Review generated QA audit evidence before any client-facing delivery.
Use public-page audit, proposal, Lighthouse, Playwright, and evidence artifacts already generated locally.
Keep authentication, payments, private client systems, and production credentials out of scope unless explicitly approved later.
reportingSchedule: Weekly and monthly drafts for Daniel review only.
nextMilestone: Review local audit, proposal, and evidence artifacts before any client delivery.
observations: Local audit, proposal, Lighthouse, Playwright, and evidence outputs are available for review.
No client feedback, satisfaction, approval, payment, or sent deliverable is recorded in local data.
nextWeekFocus: Review generated audit report and proposal for scope accuracy.
Confirm which evidence can be used in a client-facing delivery package.
Keep all client communication manual and human-approved.
riskAreas: Client approval is not recorded locally.
Payment status is not recorded locally.
Do not describe generated artifacts as sent or accepted by the client.
recommendations: Use the generated audit and proposal files as review inputs only.
Confirm engagement scope before delivery work begins.
Prepare a narrow QA Audit delivery plan before any retainer discussion.
renewalNotes: Retainer continuation should only be discussed after reviewed evidence and approved client scope exist.
Expansion should be limited to additional audit, additional coverage, automation expansion, or retainer continuation when supported by reviewed artifacts.
